description.en:
Creates an Order for processing payment transactions. Supports automatic (single-stage, set processing_mode=automatic) and manual (multi-stage, set processing_mode=manual) modes.

In automatic mode, include the transactions.payments array with the payment method. In manual mode, omit transactions and add them later via POST /v1/orders/{id}/transactions, then trigger processing with POST /v1/orders/{id}/process.

Available for: credit card, debit card, Pix (MLB), Boleto (MLB), OXXO (MLM), SPEI (MLM), PSE (MCO), Rapipago (MLA), Pago Fácil (MLA).
Unique key per order creation attempt. Prevents duplicate orders on retry.
Resource locked — a concurrent request is already processing this order
Search for orders using date range and optional filters. begin_date and end_date are required.
Returns all order information for the given order ID.
Cancels an order and all its transactions. Only orders with status=action_required or status=created can be canceled.
Conflict — order cannot be canceled in its current status

Triggers processing of an order and all its transactions. Only available for manual-mode orders (processing_mode=manual). After calling this endpoint the order transitions to processed or action_required.
Conflict — order already processed or cannot be processed in current status
Fully captures a previously authorized order (capture_mode=manual). All associated authorized transactions are captured in full.
Conflict — order cannot be captured in its current status

Performs a full or partial refund of transactions associated with an order. For a full refund, send an empty request body (omit transactions). For a partial refund, include the transactions array with the specific transaction IDs and amounts to refund.
Conflict — order cannot be refunded in its current status
Updates the payment method of a pending transaction on a manual-mode order.

title.en: Update a transaction on an order

description.en: Removes a transaction from a manual-mode order. Only available before processing.

title.en: Delete a transaction from an order

description.en: Adds a payment transaction to an order. Only available when processing_mode=manual. The order must be in status=created.

title.en: Add a transaction to an order

description.en: Request body to create an Order. Supports automatic (single-stage) and manual (multi-stage) processing modes. Use processing_mode=automatic for most integrations.
Order type. Only "online" is supported for online payments.
automatic — MP processes all transactions immediately in a single stage. manual — transactions are processed in configurable stages; use the /process endpoint to trigger processing after creation.

automatic — authorize and capture funds at the same time. manual — authorize only (reserve funds); capture later with /capture endpoint. automatic_async — order may remain in status=processing while awaiting async update; final status delivered via webhook.
Total amount to be paid as a decimal string. Must equal the sum of all payment transaction amounts. Example: "100.00".
Your internal order reference ID. Returned in all order responses.
Description of the purchased product or service.
Additional information required for specific payment methods (e.g. PSE).
IP address of the payer. Required for PSE payments.
Integration metadata used by MercadoPago internally.
Payment transactions for this order. Currently supports one transaction.
Payment transaction(s). Required when processing_mode=automatic. Must not be present when processing_mode=manual (add via POST /orders/{id}/transactions).
Transaction amount as a decimal string.
Transaction expiration in ISO 8601 duration format (e.g. "P1D" = 1 day, "PT20M" = 20 minutes). Applies to ticket and bank transfer payment methods.
Absolute expiration date-time. Takes precedence over expiration_time if both provided.
Payment method for the transaction. Use GET /v1/payment_methods to list available methods for your site.
Payment method identifier. Examples by type: Cards: visa, master, elo, hipercard, amex. Ticket/cash: bolbradesco (Boleto), oxxo, rapipago, pagofacil. Bank transfer: pix, clabe (SPEI), pse.
Card token. Required for credit_card and debit_card types.
Number of installments. Use 1 for no installments.
Text shown on payer's card statement. Maximum 50 characters.
Bank code. Required for PSE (Colombia) bank transfer payments.
Document type (e.g. CPF, CNPJ, DNI, NIT, RFC)
Payer entity type. Required for PSE (Colombia) payments.
3DS (3D Secure) configuration. After creating the order, the response indicates if a challenge is required via transactions.payments[].payment_method.transaction_security.
supported — run 3DS when supported by issuer; required — always run 3DS, reject if not supported; never — skip 3DS entirely.
Required when validation is not "never". required — only approve if liability shifts to issuer; not_required — approve regardless of liability shift outcome.
Redirect URL after PSE bank authentication completes.
Order unique identifier, automatically generated by MercadoPago.

created — order created, waiting for processing. processed — all transactions successfully processed. action_required — integrator action needed (e.g. capture an authorized payment). processing — being processed; no action needed from integrator. canceled — order canceled, will not be processed further.
accredited — payment credited; waiting_payment — waiting for payer to complete offline payment; waiting_capture — authorized payment awaiting capture; waiting_transfer — waiting for bank transfer.
Total order amount as decimal string.
Total amount effectively paid so far.
site_id of the order (e.g. MLB, MLA).
Token for client-side rendering of the payment UI (e.g. Bricks). Only returned when applicable.
Transaction ID, automatically generated by MercadoPago.
created — transaction created; processed — transaction successfully processed; action_required — integrator must act (e.g. capture); processing — awaiting asynchronous result.
URL to view or print the ticket (Boleto, OXXO, etc.).
Barcode string for offline payment methods.
Reference number for offline payment methods.
Redirect URL for PSE bank transfer (Colombia). In production, redirect the payer to this URL to complete authentication at their bank.
QR code string for Pix payments.
QR code image as Base64 for Pix payments.
End-to-end identifier for Pix transactions (mandatory tracking code).
URL to redirect payer to complete 3DS challenge (when status=pending).
Refund request for an order. Omit transactions for a full refund. Include specific transaction amounts for partial refunds.
Transactions to refund. Omit for full order refund.
Amount to refund. Omit for full transaction refund.
